auditors.finding: The current-session action ledger is already bounded to command packet outcome summaries, but no durable server-owned activity JSON packet/index exists for the final design screen.

Repo facts support a split state: current UI has a session-scoped action summary, while the dedicated audit/activity-history contour remains deferred because there is no durable server-owned activity packet/index.

No canon drift, verdict inflation, layer mix, or JSON issue was found in the spec, matrix, or audit. The only block was pre-commit closeout overclaim on head/commit/push wording, corrected before commit.

evidence.fact: The UI must consume strict JSON command packets and must not infer runtime truth from local files, logs, cached UI state, exit code alone, or narrative memory.
The UI must not parse logs as an API or synthesize healthy status from cached UI state.
stdout must be exactly one JSON object, stderr must not be parsed as truth, and previous green status must not survive invalid/missing/stale/contradictory evidence.
changed_files may be shown as audit/support signal, but it is not the UI success source.
Command API forbids plain-text parsing or log parsing fallback.
The current UI downgrades an ok action result to stale when required post-action refresh fails.
Action truth notes explicitly block runtime truth inference from running, ok, stale, timeout, invalid_json, command_error, and integration_failure display states.
The current action panel and ledger record bounded current-session command packet summaries and render changed_files count plus command-packet-outcome-only copy.
Tests verify changed_files count-only rendering, no changed path identity leak, command_error not green, failed refresh becomes stale amber, and ledger stays bounded to five rows.
Tests forbid localStorage in the UI script and relevant screen sections.

Screen 30 Audit / Activity history remains admission-required with no current web surfaces, no repo surfaces, no truth sources, no allowed UI actions, and a note requiring a bounded JSON activity packet.

The prior ledger contour intentionally added only current-session action memory with no backend history, no persisted browser ledger, no browser storage, no direct file reads, and changed_files count only.

checks.reason: The current web design UI already records bounded in-memory action summaries from command packet results.
The ledger copy and tests keep rows as command packet outcome only, with stale handling after failed refresh.
Canon, UI readiness, and command API forbid log/plain-text fallback as machine truth.
No current owner surface exposes a bounded server-owned activity JSON packet/index for the final design screen.
Existing tests forbid localStorage/sessionStorage patterns; this contour also forbids IndexedDB/browser cache as activity truth.
Current-session summaries can stay admitted, while the durable dedicated Audit / Activity History screen remains deferred pending a bounded activity packet.
